suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document an explicit evidence-only or verification-only path for rust-library-worker when the required implementation already exists in the checked-out baseline, so workers can complete fix/revalidation assignments without looking like procedure violations.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Three follow-up reviews (milestone-c-reconnect-public-id-hydration-normalization, milestone-c-reconnect-public-id-hydration-normalization-evidence-revalidation, milestone-c-typed-stored-row-record-id-decode-seam-implementation) all reported followedProcedure=false only because the correct in-scope work was validating already-landed code and backfilling evidence rather than writing new runtime changes.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify how workers should isolate feature commits in detached-head mission sessions when unrelated files are already staged, including when to stop and escalate versus when to repair staging before commit.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for milestone-c-store-schemaless-bootstrap, milestone-c-strict-record-link-string-classification, and milestone-c-strict-record-link-string-classification-get-record-proof all found commits that included unrelated pre-staged .factory artifacts alongside the intended feature change.

rejectedObservations.observation: Add a services.yaml rule for isolating mission-artifact staging from code feature commits.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected: this is normative workflow guidance for workers, not an operational command/service definition for services.yaml

rejectedObservations.observation: Apply a library fact about missing handoff/transcript artifacts for a specific worker session.
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected: this is a per-mission process traceability issue, not durable codebase knowledge for .factory/library/
